headline: A US export-control directive that pulled Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 days after launch is the dominant thread this cycle, alongside record inference-speed releases and agent-orchestration tooling.
body: A US government export-control directive forcing Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers dominates the cycle across the recommended channels [8][5][7], with the takedown landing three days after Fable 5's launch and benchmarked against Claude Opus 4.8 [5]. Non-recommended creators frame the practical response as provider independence: running models locally via Ollama and routing across Claude, DeepSeek, and others through OpenRouter [13]. Inference speed is a parallel thread, with Xiaomi and TileRT's 1-trillion-parameter MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed using FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime [4], and Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma applying diffusion to text to clear 1,000 tokens per second on H100s by shifting from memory-bound to compute-bound inference [6]. Agent tooling rounds it out: Omnigent orchestrates Claude Code, Codex, and Pi as interchangeable workers with planning, implementation, and review routing [0], while IBM Technology and Google Cloud Tech stress context engineering, MCP, and human-in-the-loop review to keep agent output reliable [3][1][12].
